expectations.text: Flags params in page.tsx typed/destructured as a plain sync object instead of Promise<...> awaited
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught this and cited async-patterns.md.

expectations.text: Flags useSearchParams() called in page.tsx with no 'use client' directive
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught the invalid client-hook-in-server-component pattern.

expectations.text: Flags RepoTokenBadge.tsx reading process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN inside a 'use client' module
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged this as a blocking secrets issue, most citing the repo's own CLAUDE.md secrets policy.

expectations.text: Suggested fixes use await params (or React.use) for F1, and either a dedicated client child or moving the value server-side for F2
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials gave concrete, correct code fixes for both issues.

expectations.text: Flags generateMetadata destructuring params via unsafe cast instead of awaiting, contrasted with the page correctly awaiting the same prop
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught this and several explicitly noted the page component does it correctly while generateMetadata does not.

expectations.text: Flags the sequential getFinding/getFindingComments/getFindingAuthor awaits as a waterfall vs Promise.all
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged this and proposed Promise.all.

expectations.text: Flags finding.createdAt (a Date) passed as a prop to the client component FindingTimeline
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught the non-serializable Date prop and proposed toISOString()/new Date() round-trip.

expectations.text: Flags handleAcknowledge (a plain non-'use server' function) being passed as onAcknowledge to a client component
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught the non-serializable function prop across the RSC boundary.

expectations.text: Flags FindingActions.tsx being an async function component while also having 'use client'
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught the invalid async client component.

expectations.text: Precision check: does not flag lib.ts's getFinding cache() wrapper as a violation
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials explicitly called this out as the correct pattern and left it alone.

expectations.text: (fixed) Precision check: does not raise a violation about the <Image> usage in page.tsx (width/height/sizes, no priority) as incorrectly configured
expectations.diagnosis_reason: FIXTURE_ISSUE fixed 2026-07-05/06: dropped the `priority` prop from the 48x48 avatar image in evals/files/finding-detail/page.tsx (no genuine hero/LCP image existed in this fixture to relocate it to, so it was simply removed). evals.json's F7 expectation text updated to match. 2-trial spot-check (not a full 5-trial rerun) confirms both trials now correctly leave the <Image> usage unflagged, explicitly citing width/height/sizes as correct and the absence of `priority` as appropriate for a non-LCP avatar, while all 5 real violations (F1-F5) and the other precision check (F6) were still caught in both trials.

expectations.text: Flags acknowledgeFinding mutating with no revalidatePath/revalidateTag afterward
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught the missing revalidation and its stale-UI consequence, several citing data-patterns.md's Server Action example directly.

expectations.text: Recommends the fix of adding revalidatePath to acknowledgeFinding, mirroring dismissFinding
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials gave the exact fix, most passing repoId through and calling revalidatePath identically to dismissFinding.

expectations.text: Precision check: does not claim dismissFinding is missing or misusing revalidatePath
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials treated dismissFinding as the correct reference implementation and did not flag it.

fixture_issues: (fixed) Eval 1 / F7: the 'correctly configured' next/image usage included `priority` on a small (48x48) avatar, which every trial reasonably questioned as an LCP-hint misuse — an ambiguous precision-trap design, not a skill deficiency. Fixed by dropping `priority` from the avatar image; 2-trial spot-check on 2026-07-06 confirms the fix (2/2 pass, all other expectations still caught).
